preview_text:
The model rates Australia as a slight favorite at 35%, with Turkey close behind at 33%. This matchup sits near parity: the underdog carries a combined 65% chance to avoid defeat, suggesting competitive underlying fundamentals in the forecast.

The win–draw–loss distribution reflects a closely contested affair. Draw probability stands at 32%, indicating a regulation stalemate remains plausible. The narrow spread across all three outcomes suggests the model sees neither side as substantially superior; marginal edges dominate the picture rather than a decisive tilt toward either team.

The market diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks assign Turkey 52% win probability, a gap of roughly 19 percentage points above the model's estimate, while Australia's market odds (21%) run 14 points lower. The model leans Australian; the consensus leans Turkish. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is better calibrated on this pair. No Polymarket estimate is currently available.
